core_mechanism: adaptive Bayesian pooling of an external information source with newly observed data through a robust two-component mixture prior, whose diffuse second component lets the borrowed prior's effective weight shrink automatically when the external source conflicts with the current data, with the whole scheme calibrated by simulation to hit target error/operating characteristics
brief_reason: The abstract's method rests on incorporating external data via analytic priors and a robust mixture prior with a weakly informative second component to mitigate bias under prior-data conflict, tuned through simulation-based control of the type I error rate.
